events.title: Lyon Classic Simca Spring Meet 2026
events.description: A full day gathering for classic Simca owners in and around Lyon. Parking, photo session, and city cruise reserved for Simca vehicles only.

events.description: Scenic road tour through the Beaujolais vineyards for classic Simca cars with picnic stops and photo opportunities.

events.description: Regional swap meet focused on French classics. Simca, Peugeot, Citroën and Renault parts welcome.

events.title: Paris Retro French Cars Day
events.description: Large gathering of classic French marques on the outskirts of Paris. Open parking with a dedicated Simca row.

events.title: Simca Track Day Nürburgring
events.description: Track day on the Nürburgring GP-Strecke for competition-prepared Simca 1000 Rallye and 1200S cars.

events.title: Barcelona Classic Simca Meet
events.description: Mediterranean beachfront meet for Simca owners across Spain and Southern France.

events.title: UK Simca Owners Weekend
events.description: Three-day camping and show weekend for UK-based Simca clubs with concours and autotest.

events.title: Grenoble Mountain Cruise
events.description: Alpine cruise into the Vercors massif, open to French classics with a strong Simca presence.

events.title: Online Tech Session: Simca 1100 Suspension Refresh
events.description: Live video workshop on refreshing and upgrading Simca 1100 front suspension and brakes.

events.title: Milan French Compact Special
events.description: Showcase of French compact cars from the 60s and 70s, including Simca 1000, Renault 8, and Peugeot 204.

car_listings.title: 1969 Simca 1200S Coupé, Italian delivery

car_listings.description: Solid Simca 1301 GLS with power front discs and original interior. Regularly driven, fully roadworthy and maintained.

car_listings.title: 1970 Simca 1301 project shell with papers

car_listings.title: 1970 Simca 1501 Special, blue, low mileage
car_listings.description: Nicely preserved Simca 1501 Special with 89k km. Strong engine, smooth gearbox, and recent clutch replacement.

car_listings.title: 1968 Simca 1501 sedan, running project
car_listings.description: Running and driving Simca 1501 with cosmetic needs. Rust in lower doors, interior tired but complete.

car_listings.title: 1978 Simca 1000 Rallye 3, German-delivered
car_listings.description: Genuine German-market Simca 1000 Rallye 3 in yellow with period roll bar and Group 2 details. Matching numbers, FIVA papers.

car_listings.title: 1961 Simca Aronde P60 Élysée
car_listings.description: Charming Aronde P60 Élysée with period-correct grey paint, original interior and recently overhauled engine.

car_listings.title: 1958 Simca Vedette Chambord V8
car_listings.description: Elegant Vedette Chambord with Ford V8, two-tone paint and lots of chrome. Older restoration still presenting well.

car_listings.title: 1949 Simca 8 barn find
car_listings.description: Barn-find Simca 8 showing lots of patina. Engine turns by hand, needs comprehensive restoration.

guides.title: Simca Aronde P60 Engine Refresh Checklist (1960–1963)
guides.summary: Step-by-step beginner-friendly checklist for refreshing the engine on a 1960–1963 Simca Aronde P60 before returning it to regular use.
guides.content: This guide walks through the essential checks and service items for waking up an Aronde P60 engine that has been sitting. We cover fluids, ignition, fuel system, compression checks, and first start procedure with photos and torque specs.

guides.title: Beginner’s Guide to Resealing the Aronde P60 Engine
guides.summary: Learn how to cure common oil leaks on the Simca Aronde P60 engine using basic tools and affordable gasket sets.
guides.content: Oil leaks are common on elderly Simca engines. In this guide we show how to change the rocker cover gasket, side cover seals, and front and rear crank seals without removing the engine from the car.

guides.title: Simca 1100 Front Brake Overhaul
guides.summary: Replace front brake pads, discs and flexi hoses on the Simca 1100 safely at home.
guides.content: A detailed photo guide covering hub removal, caliper strip-down, fitting new front brake pads and discs, and bleeding the hydraulic system. Includes torque settings and a tool checklist.

guides.title: Simca 1200S Twin Carburetor Tuning Basics
guides.summary: How to set up and synchronize the twin Weber carburetors on the Simca 1200S for smooth running and performance.
guides.content: We cover baseline jetting, checking for air leaks, synchronizing throttles with a flow meter and fine-tuning idle mixture on the 1200S. Includes example jet tables for road and track use.

guides.title: Simca 1000 Sill and Floorpan Rust Repair
guides.summary: Advanced guide covering structural rust repairs on Simca 1000 sills, jacking points and floorpans.
guides.content: This comprehensive article explains how to assess structural rust, fabricate repair sections, and weld them into the Simca 1000 shell while maintaining alignment.

guides.title: Buyer’s Guide: Simca 1301 & 1501
guides.summary: What to look for when shopping for a Simca 1301 or 1501, including rust traps, engine issues and trim availability.
guides.content: We walk you around the car and under it, highlighting typical problem areas and giving tips on verifying originality and assessing spare parts availability.

guides.title: Track Setup Tips for the Simca 1000 Rallye 3
guides.summary: Suspension, brake and engine setup suggestions for taking your Rallye 3 onto the circuit.
guides.content: Covers spring and damper choices, camber and toe settings, brake pad compounds and cooling, and safe redline limits for track-day use.

guides.title: Simca 1100 Oil Change & Basic Service
guides.summary: A quick-start guide to changing the oil and filter on the Simca 1100 and carrying out a basic annual service.
guides.content: Includes oil specification, filter cross-reference list, and a step-by-step photo guide to jacking, draining, replacing the oil filter and refilling.

guides.title: Simca Vedette Electrical System Basics
guides.summary: Understand and troubleshoot the 6V electrical system fitted to Simca Vedette models.
guides.content: We cover common charging issues, headlamp upgrades, and safe ways to convert to 12V if desired, with wiring diagrams.

forum_posts.content: I finally installed the twin Weber 40 DCOEs on my Simca 1200S and used the jetting from the workshop guide as a starting point (125 main / 200 air / 45F9 idle). Idle is OK but there is a flat spot around 2500 rpm when I open the throttle quickly. Anyone running a similar setup who can share their jet settings and ignition timing?

On my mildly tuned 1200S (stock cam, 4-2-1 exhaust) I ended up at 120 mains, 190 airs and 50F8 idles. Throttle response is much better and plugs read nicely tan. I would also check that your progression holes are properly aligned with the throttle plates at idle.

The flat spot you describe often comes from too lean progression. Try going one step richer on the idle jets before touching the mains. Also make sure you have around 3.5 psi fuel pressure only  these Webers dont like more.

What ignition timing are you running at full advance? On my car the sweet spot is about 32	0 total at 3500 rpm with vacuum disconnected. If youre too retarded there it will also feel flat even with correct jetting.
Good point about fuel pressure. I had a generic electric pump giving almost 6 psi and the car would stumble badly. A Malpassi regulator set to 3 psi transformed the throttle response.
Im using 32 mm chokes with 125 mains and 200 airs on my track-focused 1200S. For road use 30 mm chokes with 120/190 is usually nicer and gives better drivability at low rpm.

Dont forget to synchronize both carbs after any change. Use a synchrometer and aim for around 4.5.0 kg/h at idle on each throat. If one carb is doing more work than the other you will never get rid of flat spots.

I attached my last rolling road printout in the gallery. With 120/190 jets and 32 chokes the engine makes 96 hp at the wheels, AFR around 12.8:1 at full load. Thats a good target for a spirited road setup.

If you still have the stock distributor, check the advance curve. Mine had sticky weights and was only giving about 18	0 mechanical. I sent it out to be recurved and that alone made the engine much smoother through the midrange.

Update: I switched to 50F9 idle jets and set fuel pressure to 3.2 psi with a regulator. The flat spot is almost gone, only a very slight hesitation if I snap the throttle wide open in third gear. I havent touched the mains yet.

That small hesitation under sudden WOT is quite normal on these carbs without accelerator pump tweaks. You can experiment with a slightly larger pump jet (from 35 to 40) but make one change at a time so you can feel the difference.
For reference, my road setup: 30 mm chokes, 45F9 idles, 120 mains, 190 airs, F16 emulsion tubes, 35 pump jets. Starts easily hot or cold and pulls cleanly from 2000 to 7000 rpm.
Thanks everyone for the detailed advice. Ill try the 120/190 combo with 30 chokes next and play with the pump jets as suggested. Ill report back after a proper road test.
Has anyone fitted the track compound front brake pads to a standard Simca 1000 that is mostly used on the road? Im worried they wont bite when cold in city traffic.
They do need a little temperature before they really work. For a mostly road-driven car I would stay with the Ferodo-style road pads and maybe add better brake fluid and braided hoses instead.
Agree. I run the track pads only on my Rallye 2 track car. On my street 1000 the standard compound is much nicer when cold and doesnt squeal.

My Aronde P60 develops a light tapping noise after about 20 minutes of driving, especially at idle. Oil pressure is fine and the car runs well otherwise. Could this just be valve clearances or should I be more worried?

Most likely its valve clearances closing up when the engine is hot. Check and adjust them with the engine fully warm  many cars were set too tight years ago. Also make sure you are using the correct 20W-50 oil with a proper ZDDP content.
